questions.prompt: В каком предложении правильно используется 'could have' для выражения способности?

choices.text: If I had practiced more, I could have improved my score.
If I had practiced more, I might have improved my score.
If I had practiced more, I would have improved my score.
If I had practiced more, I should have improved my score.

questions.explanation: 'Could have' используется для выражения способности, тогда как другие варианты не подходят для этого контекста.

questions.prompt: Какое предложение правильно использует 'might have' для неуверенной возможности?

choices.text: If she had checked the forecast, she might have brought an umbrella.
If she had checked the forecast, she could have brought an umbrella.
If she had checked the forecast, she would have brought an umbrella.
If she had checked the forecast, she must have brought an umbrella.

questions.explanation: 'Might have' используется для неуверенной возможности, тогда как 'could have' указывает на способность.

questions.prompt: В каком случае 'could have' правильно заменяет 'would have'?

choices.text: If they had saved money, they could have bought a car.
If they had saved money, they might have bought a car.
If they had saved money, they would have bought a car.
If they had saved money, they should have bought a car.

questions.explanation: 'Could have' выражает возможность, тогда как 'would have' не подходит для этого контекста.

questions.prompt: Какое предложение правильно использует 'might have' вместо 'would have'?

choices.text: If he had left earlier, he might have caught the bus.
If he had left earlier, he could have caught the bus.
If he had left earlier, he would have caught the bus.
If he had left earlier, he must have caught the bus.

questions.explanation: 'Might have' используется для неуверенной возможности, тогда как 'would have' указывает на уверенное действие.

questions.prompt: В каком предложении правильно применяется 'could have' для способности?

choices.text: If she had the experience, she could have handled the situation.
If she had the experience, she might have handled the situation.
If she had the experience, she would have handled the situation.
If she had the experience, she should have handled the situation.

questions.explanation: 'Could have' указывает на способность, тогда как другие варианты не подходят для выражения возможности.

questions.prompt: Какой вариант содержит правильное использование 'might have'?

choices.text: If we had taken the shortcut, we might have arrived on time.
If we had taken the shortcut, we could have arrived on time.
If we had taken the shortcut, we would have arrived on time.
If we had taken the shortcut, we must have arrived on time.

questions.explanation: 'Might have' выражает неуверенность, тогда как 'could have' относится к способности.

questions.prompt: В каком случае 'could have' правильно используется вместо 'would have'?

choices.text: If I had known, I could have prevented the mistake.
If I had known, I might have prevented the mistake.
If I had known, I would have prevented the mistake.
If I had known, I should have prevented the mistake.

questions.explanation: 'Could have' используется для выражения возможности, тогда как 'would have' не подходит для этого контекста.

questions.prompt: Какое предложение правильно использует 'might have' для неуверенной возможности?

choices.text: If they had called earlier, they might have gotten a better table.
If they had called earlier, they could have gotten a better table.
If they had called earlier, they would have gotten a better table.
If they had called earlier, they must have gotten a better table.

questions.explanation: 'Might have' указывает на неуверенную возможность, тогда как 'could have' относится к способности.

questions.prompt: В каком предложении правильно применен 'could have'?

choices.text: If he had the right tools, he could have repaired the car.
If he had the right tools, he might have repaired the car.
If he had the right tools, he would have repaired the car.
If he had the right tools, he should have repaired the car.

If she had studied more, she could have passed the test.
If she had studied more, she would have passed the test.
If she had studied more, she must have passed the test.
